title: Tiffany Grant
wikitext:
[]: Tiffany Grant Post by Lonecow12 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 11:55 am Seeing that Grant will be at the Australian premiere of Eva, got me thinking about her. I still prefer Tiffany Grant's Asuka over Yūko Miyamura, but she is about the only dub voice I like. When 2.22 comes over here (in ten or so years) I wish they could make a voice option so you can switch each characters voice to Japanese or English. I think it would make sense too. I'm sure everyone in NERV would understand English, just not speak it, and Asuka being a genius, could easily understand Japanese. I'm glad Miyamura didn't attempt any German this time around though.

[]: Re: Tiffany Grant Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 15, 2010 12:07 pm lonecow wrote: When 2.22 comes over here (in ten or so years) I wish they could make a voice option so you can switch each characters voice to Japanese or English. I think it would make sense too.

[]: Re: Tiffany Grant Post by Lonecow12 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 12:11 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: But then it would sound totally stupid, like the cutscenes in Tekken 6.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 15, 2010 12:30 pm She most likely will. She's already been doing voice work for Funimation, and it would be a poor decision on their part not to use her. I know that a lot of people hate her as Asuka, but I'm sure many of them watch in sub. To a lot of people, she is Asuka, just as Spike is Shinji, and Allison is Misato. I like Tiffany, and I enjoyed her voice work in the past, but I'm just at that point where I don't care, because the dub is completely irrelevant.

[]: Post by Eva 02 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 2:36 pm On that note I get a chance to meet Tiff Grant at my local Anime Convention in October. But yeah, Eva Monkey is right, the dub is made of fail. Its exactly as if they didn't take it seriously, like they were doing a comedy and hadn't watched the whole series to see its a phych/drama. Misato's english voice is sexy but doesn't match her as good as the Japanese seiyuu. Tiffany Grant talks like a slow kuntry gurl and occasionally yodels with her petulant whining. I think if they were to do a dub now, and actually watch it subbed all the way through a few times and tried to be as true to it as possible, there could be a successful dub. But those ppl did NOT know what they were doing and have undoubtedly ruined Evangelion for a lot of ppl and dissuaded a lot of potential fans. All dub advocates must fully understand that before they go on how good the dub is. They missed the original feel of the anime including emotions and moods during important battle/drama scenes. If you haven't watched the sub you haven't even seen Evangelion, I'm sure everyone who has watched the sub twice through will agree. Of course, with Rebuild the characters are older so Shinji's screams aren't as piercing and heart-wrenching and Misato isn't as gripping and charismatic, but still better than dub alllllll day! I could have sworn I read that they were getting a new actress for Asuka, I remember being surprised because of how big a role she played in america production. Didn't they replace Rei's voice[thankfully]?

[]: Post by Dartz » Thu Jul 15, 2010 2:47 pm Reason I prefer the Dub is unrelated to the actual quality of the VA's... I like it because I can watch the screen, rather than watch the words beneath. I'm shit at languages, and would never be able to learn Japanese to the point where I could watch it Raw. I find it easier to watch... And regarding tone and mood, I find it hard enough to pick up on the tone of English speakers, a lot of the mood and tone in the sub just goes over my head. That said, Rebuild dub seemed to take a few minutes to get into the swing of things, like the VA's were trying to recover the same essence of 15 years ago. It *really* gets good though towards the end... everyone seems to catch a flow.... especially Allison Keith giving Shinji a pep talk about 'All the Power of Japan' being behind him... it seemed to stick in my mind a lot better than the original subbed version of the line. There were also a few glitches that came across as a little awkward... especially early on, but on the whole it wasn't annoying. In the series... I liked Grant's Asuka... mostly because she could pull off the polyglot thing far better than Yuko Miyamura does, time will tell about 2.0dd

[]: Post by Eva 02 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 3:11 pm ^ Yeah, the whole missing the action part is why my dad doesn't like subbed. My g/f used to be like that. When I was presenting the Eva series to her, at first she wanted to watch the dub so she wouldnt have to read so much, but I just kept bitching and bitching about it. Then come episode 3, when Shinji stabs Shamshel, I busted out laughing when I heard Spike's pathetic caterwaul. At that point I told her that its not Evangelion in the dubbed version, switched it over, and continued from there on in Japanese. She was a complete n00b to anime and even looked down on it, now she asks to watch an episode of Eva even when I dont want to, refusing to ever watch in english, no matter how drunk, tired, or dry-eyed. Its safe to say that if she had only seen the dub, that would not be so. Its something you have to practice. Sometimes[rarely] the american dub is better quality than the jap version, such as Cowboy Bebop or even... *gasp* Princess Mononoke. But in a truly effective sub, after watching the anime I should have a feeling like I watched in english. I'm an avid reader as it is, so multitasking is easy for me. I will usually not be able to see the script and animation as separate, but after viewing a sub and think back to remember parts and lines I remember them as if they were spoken by the character not read. i.e., watching mushishi and remembering a line, I can actually hear his voice talking in english, but I've only heard the jap version. Theres just an unspoiled and raw feel to subs that means you're getting the original feel of the anime. It just takes practice to reach this level of.... Self-actualization.

[]: Post by Dartz » Thu Jul 15, 2010 3:24 pm I quite liked Spike's screaming actually. He managed to sound childish when needed, and do the cold hard side when needed(ep 20). I watch fansubs as much as anyone... so I can read subs. I just prefer not to where possible.... though some VA's really do grate on me. English!Kamina makes my head spin.... The Dub cast does more than a good enough job... I find. Translations will never be perfect, but they're usually close enough for government work (People should remember how I feel about digging too deeply into things). Tiffany Grant *is* one of the better actors, and really does sound a lot more sharper and ascerbic than Yuko Miyamura (Who forever reminds me of Battle Royale girl and always sounds a little too cheerful) But then, when it comes to Shikinami, most of the people who see Shikinami as a sin before the altar Sorhyu feel the same way about Grant.

[]: Post by Eva 02 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 3:35 pm Dartz wrote: I quite liked Spike's screaming actually. He managed to sound childish when needed, and do the cold hard side when needed(ep 20).

[]: Post by Dartz » Thu Jul 15, 2010 3:42 pm Eva 02 wrote: Anta Baka ?

[]: Post by Lonecow12 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 3:45 pm It's strange how much the tables have turned in favor of Dubs in anime circles. When I started getting into anime you would be ostracized for even saying you like a dub. If you go on ANN message boards and say you like SUB better most people freak out and call you an elitist. Such a stupid trend because Subs are 90% better, and the other 10% are just equal. Of course thats all the n00bz who are wet because they met Vic Montague (whatever) and Kyle Hebert at a con and think they have to defend crappy voice actors. There are few VAs I'll go to bat for. Grant is one of them. But like Eva 02 said, it was like they didn't watch the show first. One time at a con, Grant was talking about how she met Spike once and that wasn't even while they were recording at ADV. I'm sure a lot of animation works that way, but it blows my mind that Shinji and Asuka never played off each other. Just recorded lines and left. Explains a lot as to why in the Japanese original Shinji and Asuka seem a lot closer. For one Asuka never yelled in disgust after kissing Shinji. But thats another topic.

[]: Post by Eva 02 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 4:14 pm Dartz wrote: Grant's tone with the "What are you, Stupid?" really makes you feel the condescension.

[]: Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Jul 15, 2010 4:18 pm Eva 02 wrote: I'm sure he made Megumi Ogata scream over and over again - probably why her voice sounds scratchy in Rebuild lol.

[]: Post by Eva 02 » Thu Jul 15, 2010 4:29 pm ^ Thanks for solidifying my point! lmao *In cliche Japanese voice* " Everyone: Ohhhh", "and then Anno said this", " Everyone : Ohhhhhhh!"

[]: Post by Dartz » Thu Jul 15, 2010 5:48 pm Eva 02 wrote: Do you mean: *insert a mentally slow country girl voice* "Wut arrr yoow Stooooowwwpid ? Perhaps the main reason I can't stand her. In Japanese the tone hints at "I'm surpised you're so stupid. You can't possibly not know, are you really that stupid???" Its supposed to be overly-dramatic, you see in a lot of anime people throwing around "baka," but yet you go to Japan and I'm sure the only people who say it so gratuitously are kids and teenagers.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Jul 15, 2010 7:54 pm Oh shit. Another dub fight.

[]: Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Thu Jul 15, 2010 7:56 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Oh shit. Another dub fight.
